question: The concept of 'agile software development'
options: Improving code structure without changing its external behaviour
Writing tests before writing the code that makes them pass
Separates an application into Model, View and Controller components
Iterative development with frequent delivery of working software
answer: Iterative development with frequent delivery of working software

question: 'Scrum framework' uses
options: Testing individual components of code in isolation
Short sprints, daily standups and a product backlog
Track and manage changes to code over time
That different modules work correctly when combined
answer: Short sprints, daily standups and a product backlog

question: The concept of 'DevOps'
options: Stateless, client-server architecture using standard HTTP methods
Improving code structure without changing its external behaviour
Combining development and operations for faster, more reliable software delivery
Automatically building and testing code whenever changes are committed
answer: Combining development and operations for faster, more reliable software delivery

question: 'Continuous integration' (CI)
options: Automatically building and testing code whenever changes are committed
Combining development and operations for faster, more reliable software delivery
The implied cost of future rework caused by quick, easy solutions now
Stateless, client-server architecture using standard HTTP methods
answer: Automatically building and testing code whenever changes are committed

options: Stateless, client-server architecture using standard HTTP methods
Automatically deploying tested code to production

Testing individual components of code in isolation
answer: Automatically deploying tested code to production
